existingConfigMap.description: Make use of a pre-defined configmap instead of the one templated here
Make use of a pre-defined configmap instead of the one templated here

autoscaling.description: only available if kind is Deployment

extraFiles.description:
This allows adding more files with arbitrary filenames to /fluent-bit/etc/conf by providing key/value pairs.
The key becomes the filename, the value becomes the file content.
upstream.conf: |
[UPSTREAM]
upstream1
[NODE]
name       node-1
host       127.0.0.1
port       43000
example.conf: |
[OUTPUT]
Name example
Match foo.*
Host bar

upstream.description:
https://docs.fluentbit.io/manual/administration/configuring-fluent-bit/classic-mode/upstream-servers
This configuration is deprecated, please use `extraFiles` instead.

extraPorts.description:
- port: 5170
containerPort: 5170
protocol: TCP
name: tcp
nodePort: 30517

ingress.description: only available if kind is Deployment

tls.description:
- host: fluent-bit-extra.example.tld
specify extraPort number
port: 5170

hosts:
- fluent-bit.example.tld

luaScripts.description: See Lua script configuration example in README.md

podDisruptionBudget.description: only available if kind is Deployment

autoscaling.description: only available if kind is Deployment

behavior.description:
- type: Pods
pods:
metric:
name: packets-per-second
target:
type: AverageValue
averageValue: 1k
see https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/#support-for-configurable-scaling-behavior
scaleDown:
policies:
- type: Pods
value: 4
periodSeconds: 60
- type: Percent
value: 10
periodSeconds: 60

controlledResources.description: List of resources that the vertical pod autoscaler can control. Defaults to cpu and memory

recommender.description: Name of the VPA recommender that will provide recommendations for vertical scaling.

updateMode.description:
Specifies whether recommended updates are applied when a Pod is started and whether recommended updates
are applied during the life of a Pod. Possible values are "Off", "Initial", "Recreate", and "Auto".

controlledResources.description: List of resources that the vertical pod autoscaler can control. Defaults to cpu and memory

controlledValues.description: Values that the vertical pod autoscaler can control. Allowed values are RequestsAndLimits and RequestsOnly. Default is RequestsAndLimits.

maxAllowed.description: Define the max allowed resources for the pod

minAllowed.description:
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi
Define the min allowed resources for the pod
cpu: 200m
memory: 100Mi

recommender.description: Name of the VPA recommender that will provide recommendations for vertical scaling.

updateMode.description:
Specifies whether recommended updates are applied when a Pod is started and whether recommended updates
are applied during the life of a Pod. Possible values are "Off", "Initial", "Recreate", and "Auto".

extraFiles.description:
This allows adding more files with arbitrary filenames to /fluent-bit/etc/conf by providing key/value pairs.
The key becomes the filename, the value becomes the file content.
upstream.conf: |
[UPSTREAM]
upstream1
[NODE]
name       node-1
host       127.0.0.1
port       43000
example.conf: |
[OUTPUT]
Name example
Match foo.*
Host bar

upstream.description:
https://docs.fluentbit.io/manual/administration/configuring-fluent-bit/classic-mode/upstream-servers
This configuration is deprecated, please use `extraFiles` instead.

dnsConfig.description:
nameservers:
- 1.2.3.4
searches:
- ns1.svc.cluster-domain.example
- my.dns.search.suffix
options:
- name: ndots
value: "2"
- name: edns0

envWithTpl.description:
The envWithTpl array below has the same usage as "env", but is using the tpl function to support templatable string.
This can be useful when you want to pass dynamic values to the Chart using the helm argument "--set <variable>=<value>"
https://helm.sh/docs/howto/charts_tips_and_tricks/#using-the-tpl-function
- name: FOO_2
value: "{{ .Values.foo2 }}"
foo2: bar2

extraContainers.description: This supports either a structured array or a templatable string

tag.description:
Overrides the image tag whose default is {{ .Chart.AppVersion }}
Set to "-" to not use the default value

specify extraPort number

initContainers.description: This supports either a structured array or a templatable string

luaScripts.description: See Lua script configuration example in README.md

minReadySeconds.description: How long (in seconds) a pods needs to be stable before progressing the deployment

create.description: Create SCC for Fluent-bit and allow use it

existingName.description: Use existing SCC in cluster, rather then create new one

podDisruptionBudget.description: only available if kind is Deployment

rule.description: This policy assumes the nodes are using AppArmor rather than SELinux.

podSecurityPolicy.description:
Configure podsecuritypolicy
Ref: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pod-security-policy/
from Kubernetes 1.25, PSP is deprecated
See: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2022/08/23/kubernetes-v1-25-release/#pod-security-changes
We automatically disable PSP if Kubernetes version is 1.25 or higher

enabled.description:
namespace: ""
additionalLabels: {}
rules:
- alert: NoOutputBytesProcessed
expr: rate(fluentbit_output_proc_bytes_total[5m]) == 0
annotations:
message: |
Fluent Bit instance {{ $labels.instance }}'s output plugin {{ $labels.name }} has not processed any
bytes for at least 15 minutes.
summary: No Output Bytes Processed
for: 15m
labels:
severity: critical

resources.description:
limits:
cpu: 100m
memory: 128Mi
requests:
cpu: 100m
memory: 128Mi

securityContext.description:
capabilities:
drop:
- ALL
readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
runAsNonRoot: true
runAsUser: 1000

additionalEndpoints.description:
Bear in mind if you want to collect metrics from a different port
you will need to configure the new ports on the extraPorts property.
- port: metrics
path: /metrics
interval: 10s
scrapeTimeout: 10s
scheme: ""
tlsConfig: {}
metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
metricRelabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_label_cluster]
targetLabel: cluster
regex: (.*)
replacement: ${1}
action: replace
relabel configs to apply to samples after ingestion.
relabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_node_name]
separator: ;
regex: ^(.*)$
targetLabel: nodename
replacement: $1
action: replace

Bear in mind if you want to collect metrics from a different port
you will need to configure the new ports on the extraPorts property.
- port: metrics
path: /metrics
interval: 10s
scrapeTimeout: 10s
scheme: ""
tlsConfig: {}
metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
metricRelabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_label_cluster]
targetLabel: cluster
regex: (.*)
replacement: ${1}
action: replace
relabel configs to apply to samples after ingestion.
relabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_node_name]
separator: ;
regex: ^(.*)$
targetLabel: nodename
replacement: $1
action: replace

enabled.description:
namespace: monitoring
interval: 10s
scrapeTimeout: 10s
selector:
prometheus: my-prometheus
metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion.
metricRelabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_service_label_cluster]
targetLabel: cluster
regex: (.*)
replacement: ${1}
action: replace
relabel configs to apply to samples after ingestion.
relabelings:
- sourceLabels: [__meta_kubernetes_pod_node_name]
separator: ;
regex: ^(.*)$
targetLabel: nodename
replacement: $1
action: replace
scheme: ""
tlsConfig: {}

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: How long (in seconds) a pod may take to exit (useful with lifecycle hooks to ensure lb deregistration is done)

volumeMounts.description: The config volume is mounted by default, either to the existingConfigMap value, or the default of "fluent-bit.fullname"
